description: LoadUser uses Optional.orElseGet with a Supplier lambda (lazy); LoadTag and LoadLabel use Optional.orElse with literal defaults (eager). Unifying onto orElse re-introduces the ghost-user bug — provisionNewUser would be invoked on every cache hit.

findings.rationale: loadOrProvision calls orElseGet with a lambda (FUNCTION inside CALL); the other two siblings call orElse with a simple member access. Same call-chain shape, divergent callee + argument complexity. May hit label-divergence (same shape, different IDENT_CALLEE) or structural-unclassified due to the embedded FUNCTION.
